title: C++ STL Mastery
readme:
Here we go, ---

# **Day 01 \u2014 C++ STL Mastery (README.rst)**  
*30 Days to DSA in C++ \u2014 Ultra\u2011Premium Edition*

---

## **1. Introduction**

The C++ Standard Template Library (STL) is one of the most powerful components of modern C++.  
It provides ready\u2011made, optimized, generic implementations of the most common data structures and algorithms.

Mastering STL is not optional \u2014 it is the foundation for writing clean, fast, and interview\u2011ready C++ code.

Today\u2019s goal is to build a deep, intuitive, and practical understanding of:

- STL Containers  
- Iterators  
- Algorithms  
- Function objects (functors)  
- Lambda expressions  
- Best practices for competitive programming and interviews  

---

## **2. Why STL Matters**

STL gives you:

- **Speed** \u2014 highly optimized, often better than hand\u2011written code  
- **Safety** \u2014 fewer bugs, fewer memory errors  
- **Productivity** \u2014 solve problems faster  
- **Consistency** \u2014 predictable behavior across platforms  
- **Interview advantage** \u2014 reduces implementation time drastically  

If you know STL well, you can solve 80% of DSA problems faster and cleaner.

---

## **3. STL Components Overview**

STL has 4 major parts:

### **3.1 Containers**
Data structures that store elements.

- `vector`
- `array`
- `deque`
- `list`
- `forward_list`
- `stack`
- `queue`
- `priority_queue`
- `set`
- `unordered_set`
- `map`
- `unordered_map`
- `multiset`
- `multimap`

---

### **3.2 Iterators**
Generalized pointers used to traverse containers.

Types:
- Input iterator  
- Output iterator  
- Forward iterator  
- Bidirectional iterator  
- Random access iterator  

---

### **3.3 Algorithms**
Pre\u2011built functions for searching, sorting, counting, transforming, etc.

Examples:
- `sort()`
- `binary_search()`
- `lower_bound()`
- `upper_bound()`
- `reverse()`
- `accumulate()`
- `max_element()`
- `min_element()`
- `count()`
- `find()`

---

### **3.4 Functors & Lambdas**
Custom logic passed into STL algorithms.

---

## **4. Deep Dive into Important Containers**

---

# **4.1 vector \u2014 The Most Important STL Container**

### **Key Features**
- Dynamic array  
- Random access  
- Amortized O(1) insertion at end  
- O(n) insertion in middle  
- Contiguous memory  

### **Common Operations**
```cpp
vector<int> v;
v.push_back(10);
v.push_back(20);
v.pop_back();
v.size();
v.empty();
v.clear();
```

### **Iterating**
```cpp
for (int x : v) cout << x << " \
theory:
---

# **Day 1 \u2013 THEORY.rst**  
## **\ud83d\ude80 C++ STL Mastery \u2013 Containers, Iterators, Algorithms, Patterns**

---

# **\ud83c\udf1f 1. Overview**

Day 1 launches your 30\u2011day DSA journey with one of the most powerful tools in modern C++:

# **\ud83e\uddf0 The Standard Template Library (STL)**

STL gives you:

- \u26a1 Fast, optimized data structures  
- \ud83e\udde0 Ready\u2011made algorithms  
- \ud83d\udd04 Iterators for seamless traversal  
- \ud83e\uddf1 Reusable components  
- \ud83d\ude80 Competitive\u2011programming\u2011level speed  

Mastering STL is like unlocking **superpowers** in C++.

---

# **\ud83d\udce6 2. STL Components (The Holy Trinity)**

STL is built on **three pillars**:

---

## **1\ufe0f\u20e3 Containers (Data Structures)**  
Hold data.

Examples:  
- \ud83d\udfe6 `vector`  
- \ud83d\udfe9 `deque`  
- \ud83d\udfe8 `list`  
- \ud83d\udfe5 `stack`  
- \ud83d\udfea `queue`  
- \ud83d\udfe7 `priority_queue`  
- \ud83d\udfeb `set`, `unordered_set`  
- \ud83d\udfe9 `map`, `unordered_map`  

---

## **2\ufe0f\u20e3 Iterators (Pointers on Steroids)**  
Traverse containers.

Types:  
- \u27a1 Forward  
- \u2194 Bidirectional  
- \ud83d\udd01 Random access  

---

## **3\ufe0f\u20e3 Algorithms (Pre\u2011built Logic)**  
Perform operations like:

- \ud83d\udd0d Searching  
- \ud83d\udd04 Sorting  
- \ud83e\uddf9 Removing  
- \ud83e\uddee Counting  
- \ud83e\udde9 Transforming  

Examples:  
`sort`, `reverse`, `count`, `accumulate`, `lower_bound`, `upper_bound`, `binary_search`

---

# **\ud83e\uddf1 3. Containers \u2013 Deep Dive**

---

## **\ud83d\udfe6 3.1 vector \u2013 The Workhorse**

### **Why it\u2019s amazing**
- Dynamic resizing  
- Random access  
- Cache\u2011friendly  
- Fast for push_back  

### **Key operations**
- `push_back()`  
- `pop_back()`  
- `size()`  
- `capacity()`  
- `reserve()`  
- `shrink_to_fit()`  

### **Time complexities**
- Access: **O(1)**  
- Insert at end: **Amortized O(1)**  
- Insert in middle: **O(n)**  

---

## **\ud83d\udfe9 3.2 deque \u2013 Double\u2011Ended Vector**

### **Why it\u2019s useful**
- Fast push/pop at **both ends**  
- Used in sliding window problems  

### **Time complexities**
- push_front: **O(1)**  
- push_back: **O(1)**  
- random access: **O(1)**  

---

## **\ud83d\udfe8 3.3 list \u2013 Doubly Linked List**

### **Why it\u2019s useful**
- Fast insert/delete anywhere  
- Stable iterators  

### **Time complexities**
- Insert/delete: **O(1)**  
- Access: **O(n)**  

---

## **\ud83d\udfe5 3.4 stack \u2013 LIFO**

Built on vector/deque/list.

Used in:  
- Parentheses problems  
- DFS  
- Expression evaluation  

---

## **\ud83d\udfea 3.5 queue \u2013 FIFO**

Used in:  
- BFS  
- Level order traversal  
- Scheduling  

---

## **\ud83d\udfe7 3.6 priority_queue \u2013 Max Heap**

### **Why it\u2019s powerful**
- Always gives largest element  
- Used in Dijkstra, heaps, scheduling  

### **Min\u2011heap trick**
```
priority_queue<int, vector<int>, greater<int>> pq;
```

---

## **\ud83d\udfeb 3.7 set / unordered_set**

### **set**
- Balanced BST  
- Sorted  
- O(log n) operations  

### **unordered_set**
- Hash table  
- O(1) average  
- Not sorted  

---

## **\ud83d\udfe9 3.8 map / unordered_map**

### **map**
- Sorted keys  
- O(log n)  

### **unordered_map**
- Hash table  
- O(1) average  
- Most used in coding interviews  

---

# **\ud83e\udded 4. Iterators \u2013 The Glue of STL**

Iterators behave like pointers.

### **Types**
- `begin()`  
- `end()`  
- `rbegin()`  
- `rend()`  
- `cbegin()`  
- `cend()`  

### **Common patterns**
```
for (auto it = v.begin(); it != v.end(); it++)
```

---

# **\u2699\ufe0f 5. STL Algorithms \u2013 The Real Power**

---

## **\ud83d\udfe6 5.1 Sorting**

```
sort(v.begin(), v.end());
```

### **Custom comparator**
```
sort(v.begin(), v.end(), [](int a, int b){
    return a > b;
});
```

---

## **\ud83d\udfe9 5.2 Searching**

### **Binary search**
```
binary_search(v.begin(), v.end(), x);
```

### **lower_bound**
First element >= x

### **upper_bound**
First element > x

---

## **\ud83d\udfe8 5.3 Useful algorithms**

- `reverse()`  
- `max_element()`  
- `min_element()`  
- `accumulate()`  
- `count()`  
- `unique()`  
- `erase()`  

---

# **\ud83e\udde0 6. STL Patterns You Must Memorize**

---

## **Pattern 1 \u2014 Frequency Map**
```
unordered_map<int,int> mp;
for (int x : a) mp[x]++;
```

---

## **Pattern 2 \u2014 Sorting Pairs**
```
vector<pair<int,int>> v;
sort(v.begin(), v.end());
```

---

## **Pattern 3 \u2014 Using priority_queue for k largest**
```
priority_queue<int> pq;
```

---

## **Pattern 4 \u2014 Using set for unique sorted values**
```
set<int> s(a.begin(), a.end());
```

---

## **Pattern 5 \u2014 Two\u2011pointer with vector**
```
int i = 0, j = n-1;
while (i < j) { ... }
```

---

# **\ud83d\udcd8 7. Time & Space Complexity of STL Containers**

| Container | Access | Insert | Delete | Search |
|----------|--------|--------|--------|--------|
| vector | O(1) | O(n) | O(n) | O(n) |
| deque | O(1) | O(1) | O(1) | O(n) |
| list | O(n) | O(1) | O(1) | O(n) |
| set | O(log n) | O(log n) | O(log n) | O(log n) |
| unordered_set | O(1) avg | O(1) avg | O(1) avg | O(1) avg |
| map | O(log n) | O(log n) | O(log n) | O(log n) |
| unordered_map | O(1) avg | O(1) avg | O(1) avg | O(1) avg |

---

# **\ud83c\udfaf 8. Interview\u2011Level Insights**

- STL is optimized in C++ and faster than custom implementations  
- Prefer `unordered_map` over `map` unless ordering matters  
- Prefer `vector` over `list` unless frequent middle insertions  
- Use `priority_queue` for top\u2011k problems  
- Use `set` for sorted unique values  
- Use `lower_bound` for binary search patterns  

---

# **\ud83c\udfc1 9. Conclusion**

Day 1 gives you the **foundation** for the entire 30\u2011day journey.

You now understand:

- STL containers  
- Iterators  
- Algorithms  
- Patterns  
- Time complexities  

This knowledge will be used **every single day** in the upcoming topics.
